name: Bitcoin Infrastructure Acquisition Corp Ltd
business: Bitcoin Infrastructure Acquisition Corp Ltd (BIXI) is a special purpose acquisition company formed to effect a merger or similar business combination with one or more businesses, focusing on companies operating in the digital asset space. As of the filing, it has not identified any specific business combination target and has not engaged in substantive discussions with any target. The company intends to leverage its management team's experience in digital financial infrastructure to identify and acquire a target business.

exposures.sentence: Our efforts to identify a potential initial business combination target will focus on companies operating in the digital asset space.

building core infrastructure such as wallets, custody, exchanges, lending protocols, and tokenized financial instruments as well as real-world applications of blockchain and distributed ledger technologies, including in payments, DeFi, and cross-border finance.

If we pursue a business combination with a company that relies on, or is heavily involved in the development or application of, artificial intelligence ( AI ) we may face risks in and challenges to data integrity and security, regulatory risks, technical and operational risks.

tamClaims.claim: Total USD stablecoin supply grew from $11.9 billion in July 2020 to $261.6 billion in July 2025, an 85% CAGR.
tamClaims.sentence: total supply is growing at an 85% CAGR from $11.9 billion in July 2020 to $261.6 billion in July 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Total USD stablecoin float could exceed $1 trillion by 2028, an additional 60% CAGR from today.
tamClaims.sentence: propel total USD stablecoin float beyond $1 trillion by 2028, an additional 60% CAGR from today

tamClaims.claim: BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) reached $70 billion in assets under management less than a year after launch.
tamClaims.sentence: BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) is officially the fastest-growing exchange-traded fund in history, reaching $70 billion in assets under management less than a year after launch.
